1.1: Paulus, an Apostle of Yeshua The Messiah, by the commandment of God Our Lifegiver, and of The Messiah Yeshua, our hope,
1.2: To Timotheus, a true son in the faith: Grace, mercy and peace from God Our Father and The Messiah Yeshua, Our Lord.
1.3: I requested of you when I went to Macedonia that you remain in Ephesaus and that you command certain men that they would not teach diverse doctrines,
1.4: And that they would not give heed to fables and to accounts of endless genealogies; these things produce all the more contentions and not edification in the faith of God.
1.5: But the sum of The Commandments is love from a pure heart, from a good conscience and from true faith.
1.6: Some of them have strayed from these things and have turned people away to empty words,
1.7: In that they seek to be teachers of The Written Law, while they do not understand anything that they say, neither anything about which they contend.
1.8: But we know that The Written Law is good if a man will be led by it, according to The Written Law,
1.9: While he knows that The Written Law was not appointed for the righteous, but for the evil, the rebellious, the wicked, for sinners, for the vicious, for those who are impure, for those who strike their fathers, those who strike their mothers, for murderers,
1.10: For fornicators, for males who lie down with males, for kidnappers of free men, for liars, for oath breakers and for all things opposed to the sound teaching
1.11: Of The Good News of the glory of the Blessed God, with which I have been entrusted.
1.12: And I thank him who has empowered me, our Lord Yeshua The Messiah, who accounted me trustworthy and has put me into his ministry,
1.13: Me, I who from the first was a blasphemer and a persecutor and abusive, but I obtained mercy, because I did not know what I was doing, without faith.
1.14: But the grace of our Lord abounded in me, and faith and love, which are in Yeshua The Messiah.
1.15: This is a trustworthy saying and worthy of acceptance: “Yeshua The Messiah came to the universe to save sinners, of whom I am the foremost.”
1.16: But because of this he showed me mercy, that Yeshua The Messiah might show in me all his longsuffering, as the example of those who are going to believe in him unto eternal life.
1.17: But to The King of the universe, to him who is indestructible and unseen, who is The One God, be honor and glory to the eternity of eternities. Amen.
1.18: I commit this decree to you, my son, Timotheus, according to the former prophecies which were concerning you, that you may wage this excellent warfare by them,
1.19: In faith and in good conscience, for those who have rejected this from themselves have been emptied of faith,
1.20: Like Humenayus and Alexandrus, those whom I have delivered to Satan that they may be instructed not to blaspheme.

2.1: Therefore, I beg of you that before all things, you will offer supplications to God, prayers, intercessions and thanksgiving, for the sake of all people,
2.2: For the sake of Kings and Rulers, that we may lead a quiet and tranquil way of life in complete reverence to God and purity.
2.3: For this is pleasing and acceptable before God Our Lifegiver,
2.4: He who wills that all people shall have Life, and shall be converted to the knowledge of the truth.
2.5: For God is One, and The Mediator of God and the sons of men is One: The Son of Man, Yeshua The Messiah,
2.6: Who gave himself a ransom in the place of every person, a testimony that has come in its time,
2.7: That of which I am appointed a Preacher and an Apostle of the truth. I say and I do not lie, that I am the Teacher of the Gentiles in the belief of the truth.
2.8: I desire, therefore, that men would pray in every place, lifting their hands in purity without rage and without scheming;
2.9: Likewise also the women shall be modest in fashion of dress, their adornment shall be in bashfulness and in modesty, not in braiding with gold or with pearls or in gorgeous robes,
2.10: But with good works, just as it is suitable for women who profess the worship of God.
2.11: Let women learn in silence with all submission,
2.12: For I do not allow a woman to teach, neither to usurp over a man, but she should be quiet;
2.13: For Adam was formed first, and then Eve.
2.14: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman was deceived and violated the commandment;
2.15: But she lives by her children, if they continue in faith and in love, in holiness and in modesty.

3.1: This is a trustworthy saying, that if a man desires Eldership, he desires a good work.
3.2: And an Elder ought to be one in whom no fault is found and is the husband of one woman, is of a vigilant mind, sober, orderly, loves strangers and is a teacher;
3.3: And he does not transgress concerning wine and his hand is not swift to strike, but he should be humble, not contentious, neither a lover of money,
3.4: And he governs his house well, and holds his children in subjection with all purity.
3.5: For if he does not know how to lead his own household well, how can he lead the Church of God?
3.6: Neither should he be a young disciple, lest he be lifted up and would fall into the judgment of Satan;
3.7: He ought to have an excellent testimony from outsiders, lest he fall into shame and into the trap of Satan.
3.8: In this way also, Ministers should be pure and should not double-speak, neither be inclined to much wine, neither love defiled riches,
3.9: But they should hold the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.
3.10: And those who are such should be first proved and then let them serve, being without fault.
3.11: In this way also, the wives should be modest and alert in their minds and shall be faithful in everything and should not be slanderers.
3.12: A Minister should be one who has one wife and leads his children and his household well.
3.13: For those who serve well purchase a good rank for themselves and much boldness in the faith of Yeshua The Messiah.
3.14: I have written these things to you, as I hope that I shall soon come to you,
3.15: But if I delay, that you may know how it is necessary to behave in the household of God, which is the church of THE LIVING GOD, the pillar and foundation of the truth.
3.16: And this Mystery of Righteousness is truly great, which was revealed in the flesh and was justified in The Spirit; He appeared to Angels and was preached among the Gentiles; He was trusted in the world and he ascended into glory.

4.1: But The Spirit speaks plainly that in the last time they shall depart one by one from the faith and they shall go after deceiving spirits and after the teachings of demons,
4.2: These persons who deceive by false appearances and speak lies and sear their consciences;
4.3: And they forbid to be married and they abstain from foods which God has created for use and thanksgiving for those who believe and know the truth.
4.4: For every creation is beautiful to God, and there is nothing to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving,
4.5: For it is sanctified by the word of God and by prayer.
4.6: If you will teach your brethren these things, you will be a good Minister of Yeshua The Messiah, as you have been brought up in the words of faith and in the good teaching with which you have been taught.
4.7: But abstain from insipid fables of old women and train yourself in righteousness;
4.8: For exercise of the body profits a little for this time, but righteousness profits in everything, and it has the promise of life for this time and of the future.
4.9: This is a trustworthy saying and is worthy of acceptance.
4.10: For because of this we toil and we are reproached, because we hope in THE LIVING GOD, who is the Lifegiver of all of the children of men, especially of believers.
4.12: Let no man despise your youth, but be an example to believers in word and in behavior, in love, in faith and in purity.
4.13: Until I come, be diligent in reading, in prayer and in teaching.
4.14: Do not despise the gift that is in you, which is given to you by prophecy and by the laying on of the hands of the Eldership.
4.15: Meditate on these things and abide in them, that you may be known to every person before whom you come.
4.16: Pay attention to your soul and to your teachings; persevere in them, for when you do these things you will bring life to your soul and to those who hear you.

5.1: Do not rebuke an elder, but petition him as a father, and those who are young as your brothers,
5.2: And elder women as mothers, and those who are younger, as your sisters in all purity.
5.3: Honor widows who are widows indeed;
5.4: And if there is a widow who has children or grandchildren, let them learn first that they should be assigned to children of their households, and they should pay compensation to their parents, for this is acceptable before God.
5.5: But whoever is truly a widow and is alone, her hope is upon God and continues in prayer and in supplication, night and day;
5.6: But she who worships the Arena is dead while she lives.
5.7: You shall command these things to them that they might be without fault.
5.8: For if a man does not take care of those who are his own, especially those who are members of the household of faith, this one has renounced the faith and is worse than those who are unbelievers.
5.9: Therefore you shall choose a widow who is not less than sixty years old, who had one husband,
5.10: And she has a testimony of good works, if she has raised children, if she has received strangers, if she has washed the feet of the Saints, if she has relieved the afflicted, if she has walked in every good work.
5.11: But avoid those widows who are younger, for these separate from The Messiah and seek to have a husband;
5.12: And their judgment is set, for they have rejected their former faith;
5.13: But they learn laziness while going around house to house, and not only laziness, but also to increase talking, to take up vanities and speak that which is inappropriate.
5.14: I am willing therefore that the younger ones marry, bear children, lead their households and do not give any occasion of contempt to the enemies;
5.15: For even now they have begun, one by one, to turn after Satan.
5.16: If male or female believers have widows, let them support them, and do not let them burden the church, so that it may have enough for those who are truly widows.
5.17: Those Elders who lead well deserve double honor, especially those who toil in the word and in teaching.
5.18: For the Scriptures say, “Do not muzzle the ox while it is treading”, and, “The laborer deserves his wages.”
5.19: Do not accept an accusation against an Elder, but upon the mouth of two or three witnesses;
5.20: Those who sin, rebuke before everyone, so that the rest of the people may fear.
5.21: But I testify to you before God and our Lord Yeshua The Messiah and his chosen Angels, that you will observe these things and not let your mind be prejudiced for anything and do nothing by favoritism.
5.22: Lay hands quickly on no man, and do not share in the sins of strangers; keep yourself in purity.
5.23: Do not drink water from now on, but drink a little wine because of your stomach and because of your chronic illness.
5.24: There are people whose sins are known, and they precede them to the place of judgment, and some follow them.
5.25: In this way also, good works are known, and those which are otherwise cannot be concealed.

6.1: Let those who are under the yoke of bondage hold their masters in all honor that the name of God and his teaching be not blasphemed.
6.2: Let those who have believing masters not despise them, because they are their brothers, but let them rather serve them, because they are believers and beloved, by whom they are at peace in their service. Teach these things and implore them.
6.3: But if there is a man who teaches another doctrine and does not attain to the sound words of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah and the teaching of the reverence of God,
6.4: This one is lifted up as he knows nothing, but he is ineffective in disputes and debates of words, from which come envy, contention and blasphemy, and establishes evil thoughts,
6.5: And the misery of the children of men who corrupt their minds and are cheated of the truth, and they think that making money is the worship of God; but stay away from these things,
6.6: For our profit is great, which is the worship of God while having the necessities, for we have enough.
6.7: For we have brought nothing into the universe, and we know that we can take nothing out of it;
6.8: Because of this, food and clothing is enough for us.
6.9: But those who desire to be rich fall into temptations and into traps, and into many foolish and harmful desires, and they sink the children of men into corruption and destruction.
6.10: But the root of all these evils is the love of money, and there are some who have desired it and have erred from the faith and have brought themselves many miseries.
6.11: But you, Oh man of God, escape from these things, and run after righteousness, after justice, after faith, after love, after patience and after humility.
6.12: And fight in the good contest of faith and seize eternal life, to which things you are called, and you have professed a good profession before many witnesses.
6.13: I testify to you before God, he who gives life to all, and Yeshua The Messiah, he who testified an excellent testimony before Pontius Pilate,
6.14: That you keep the commandments without defilement and without blemish until the revelation of our Lord Yeshua The Messiah,
6.15: He who is going to reveal himself in his time, The Blessed God and Only Mighty One, The King of Kings and the Lord of Power,
6.16: He who alone is uncorrupted and dwelling in light that a human cannot approach and which none of the children of men has seen, nor can see, to whom be honor and authority to the eternity of eternities. Amen.
6.17: I command the rich of this world that they not be lifted up in their minds and that they would not trust upon wealth because there is no certainty concerning it, but upon THE LIVING GOD, who gives richly to us all for our comfort,
6.18: And that they should do good works, should be rich in good deeds, and should be ready to give and to share,
6.19: And they shall lay up for themselves a good foundation for what is future, that they may seize upon true life.
6.20: Oh, Timothy, guard that which is committed to you and escape from empty echoes and from the perversions of false knowledge.
6.21: For those who profess it have themselves gone astray from the faith. Grace be with you. Amen.
